aliases: Iced Nanyang Coffee with Gula Melaka

english: Iced Nanyang Coffee with Gula Melaka

food_subgroup: Hot and cold coffee beverages with sweeteners

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301710) but no specific entry for iced kopi with gula melaka (palm sugar syrup). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers coffee as consumed. No specific code exists for iced kopi with gula melaka; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731007 refers to coffee as a substance. No specific concept for iced kopi melaka; closest parent used.

preparation_method: sweetened with gula melaka (palm sugar syrup)
mixed with evaporated or condensed milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of Ice Kopi Melaka as served at Toast Box, approximately 400 ml, containing brewed Nanyang robusta coffee, gula melaka palm sugar syrup, evaporated or condensed milk, and ice.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, with milk and sugar, prepared — adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central reference values for sweetened iced coffee with palm sugar
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Ice Kopi Melaka (Large) exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by component analysis: Nanyang robusta kopi (brewed, ~5 kcal/100 ml base), gula melaka palm sugar syrup (~60 g sugar per 100 g syrup, contributing approximately 15–20 g sugar per 400 ml serving), and evaporated milk (~135 kcal/100 ml, ~6.8 g fat, ~9.7 g carbohydrate per 100 ml). A large 400 ml serving is estimated to contain approximately 120–140 kcal total. Per-100 ml values are derived from this component estimate. HPB Foodo 'Kopi C' and 'Kopi with condensed milk' entries used as reference anchors. USDA FoodData Central NDB 14209 (Coffee, brewed) used for base coffee values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugars and sweetened beverages — palm sugar (gula melaka) GI reference; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ice Kopi Melaka. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) gula melaka (palm sugar) has a reported GI of approximately 35–54 per University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for palm sugar and coconut sugar; (2) condensed/evaporated milk contributes lactose (GI ~46); (3) the blended beverage GI is estimated at 55 (low) as a conservative composite. Glycemic load per 400 ml serving calculated as: (5.5 g carbohydrate per 100 ml × 4 × 55) / 100 = 12.1 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Ice Kopi Melaka contains approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 ml, posing minimal sodium load for hypertensive individuals, though the caffeine content from robusta coffee may cause a transient and modest rise in blood pressure; moderate consumption of one large cup per day is generally acceptable for most hypertensive patients not sensitive to caffeine.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added gula melaka (palm sugar) and condensed milk contribute significant free sugars and a medium glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: A large serving of Ice Kopi Melaka delivers approximately 22 g of free sugars and a glycemic load of approximately 12, primarily from gula melaka palm sugar syrup and condensed or evaporated milk; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption to occasional servings and consider requesting reduced sugar or substituting with a sugar-free alternative to manage postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and low sodium; moderate free sugar content warrants attention in high-frequency consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ice Kopi Melaka is relatively low in saturated fat (approximately 0.6 g per 100 ml) and sodium, presenting a low direct cardiovascular risk per serving; however, regular daily consumption of large sweetened coffee drinks contributes to free sugar intake that may adversely affect triglyceride levels and cardiovascular risk over time, and individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit added-sugar beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice Kopi Melaka (Large) is a chilled Singaporean kopitiam-style iced coffee beverage made with Nanyang robusta coffee (kopi), gula melaka (palm sugar syrup derived from the Arenga pinnata or coconut palm), evaporated or condensed milk, and ice. It is a signature menu item at Toast Box, a Singaporean café chain, and is served in a large cup of approximately 400 ml. The beverage is characterised by a rich, slightly smoky robusta coffee base balanced by the distinctive caramel-like, earthy sweetness of gula melaka, which differs from refined white sugar in flavour complexity and has a moderately lower glycemic index. The milk component adds creaminess and a mild dairy note. Nutritionally, a large serving provides approximately 130–140 kcal, with the majority of calories derived from free sugars (gula melaka and condensed milk) and a small contribution from fat and protein from the milk. The beverage has a low glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 12 per large serving, making it a moderate glycemic concern for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is low. The drink is halal-certified in the context of Toast Box and is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to the dairy component. It is a culturally significant beverage representing the Nanyang coffee tradition of Singapore and Malaysia, where robusta beans are roasted with butter and sugar and brewed through a sock filter.

meal_context: accompaniment to kaya toast or soft-boiled eggs

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Ice Kopi Melaka (Large) was found in HPB Foodo or any primary approved nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using HPB Foodo reference entries for kopi variants (Kopi C, Kopi with condensed milk) and USDA FoodData Central values for brewed coffee and evaporated milk. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for palm sugar and lactose-containing dairy. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 ml (treating the beverage as approximately 1 g/ml density), estimated from component analysis. A large serving is approximately 400 ml / 400 g. 2. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids passing into the final drink; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo analogous kopi entries. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as no hydrogenated fats are used in traditional kopi preparation. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; coffee and dairy do not contribute meaningful vitamin C. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 13. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the reference sources used. 15. glycemic_index set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in approved sources. 16. Serving size of 400 ml is an estimate for a 'large' cup at Toast Box; actual volume may vary. 17. Sugar content may vary depending on the amount of gula melaka syrup added by the outlet.
